observations: HIGH FIX (exception-set equality) VERIFIED CLOSED, THREE INDEPENDENT WAYS: (1) ran the contract's own new entry 5 ('MECHANISM -- EXCEPTION-SET EQUALITY, BOTH DIRECTIONS') verbatim, extracted via json.load (not transcribed) from .agent/contracts/043.json, in a fresh disposable cp -a clone -- printed PASS, and its internal drift sub-scenario correctly raised AssertionError('unexpected extra exception(s)', {'.agent/contracts/999-drift.json', ...}) before being caught by the entry's own || fallback, exactly as the entry's design intends. (2) Independently reconstructed round-0's exact drift scenario from scratch (fresh EXEC_ID, fresh disposable clone, an untracked .agent/contracts/999-drift.json tolerated only at the approved step, approver 'alice.chen') and ran the equality check directly (not the contract's embedded copy): correctly raised AssertionError naming the drifted path -- confirms the fix is not merely self-serving inside the contract's own proof. (3) CARVE-OUT PROBE (requested): constructed an approved-step tolerate-list with TWO unauthorized extras simultaneously present in the untracked-diff set -- the legitimate, allowed self-reference evidence-directory path AND a genuine, never-presented drift file (.agent/contracts/888-extra.json). The check correctly still flagged the illegitimate extra (AssertionError naming both items in the raw set-difference, since 'aset - pset <= allowed' fails whenever ANY member of the difference is outside 'allowed', regardless of how many legitimate members are also present) -- the evidence-dir carve-out does not create a blind spot for a second, unrelated extra riding alongside it.

SUBSET DIRECTION (requested, not explicitly covered by the contract's own entry-5 self-test) VERIFIED CAUGHT: constructed a scenario where the operator was presented two pending exceptions (.agent/contracts/043.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) but the approved-step invocation's exceptions[] records only one of them (simulated by deleting v0.6.0-release-notes.md before the approved step ran, so it no longer appears as untracked at all) -- the approved step still returns exit 3, PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, with a strict-subset exceptions[] ({'.agent/contracts/043.json', '.agent/evidence/<id>/'}). The check's second assertion, '(aset - allowed) == pset' (exact equality, not merely a subset test), correctly raised AssertionError('approved set (minus self-reference dir) != pending set', ...) on this omission. This confirms the fix structurally closes both directions of drift (silent addition AND silent omission) via the same exact-equality assertion, not merely the addition direction round 0 originally demonstrated.

MOVE-SEMANTICS FIX VERIFIED, BOTH DIRECTIONS: ran the contract's own new entry 4 ('MECHANISM -- MOVE SEMANTICS') verbatim -- PASS, confirming a correct move leaves .agent/evidence/<id>/approved/ with only collection-log.txt and both schema-valid artifacts land in .agent/reconciliation/<id>/. Reverse-direction probe (requested): manually constructed the post-implementation directory layout with a residual DUPLICATE preflight.json/baseline.json left behind in .agent/evidence/<id>/approved/ (simulating a generator that used cp instead of mv, i.e. exactly the round-0 MEDIUM defect), then ran entry 15's literal pinning logic ('GATE-DEPENDENT ... NO DUPLICATE VERDICT ARTIFACTS') against it -- correctly failed with 'duplicate preflight.json unexpectedly present under .agent/evidence/<id>/approved/ -- should have been MOVED, not copied'. The MEDIUM contradiction is resolved consistently across design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure (now reads MOVES), design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts (marked RESOLVED, NO DUPLICATION), scope.files_create (still correctly omits the approved/ raw preflight.json/baseline.json as deliverables), and is now pinned by both a mechanism-proof (entry 4) and a GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation counterpart (entry 15) in both directions (correct-move passes; residual-duplicate fails).

FULL 19-ENTRY SWEEP CONFIRMS THE GENERATOR'S OWN CLAIMED SPLIT: extracted and executed all 19 verification commands verbatim (via json.load, never transcribed) against a single fresh disposable cp -a clone of the real repository pre-implementation. Entries 1-10 (anti-vacuity + all mechanism/structural proofs, HEAD-anchored, self-contained in disposable fixtures) all PASS (rc=0). Entries 11-16 (the six GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation checks, including the two new ones -- exception-set equality and no-duplicate-verdict-artifacts) all correctly FAIL pre-implementation with clear 'not found yet' / 'not both present' messages (rc=1), matching the established anti-vacuity pattern from prior features (038-042) exactly -- this is expected and correct, not a defect, since no real dogfood run has occurred yet. Entries 17-19 (non-goal enforcement, scope guard, immutability guard) all PASS since no out-of-scope surface is touched yet. This exactly matches the generator's own claimed '13 mechanism-pass now, 6 gate-dependent correctly pending' (10 + 3 = 13; 6 gate-dependent).

A1 (sentinel-blocklist fabrication vector, Mode A) CARRIES UNCHANGED: SENTINELS = {'dry-run-mechanism-proof','test-operator','test','jane',''} is byte-identical to round 0; a plausible non-sentinel string ('alice.chen', used throughout this round's own adversarial fixtures) still passes undetected, as it must given no independent, orchestrator-authored record exists to cross-check against. No stronger contract-level binding has been introduced or is newly achievable in this revision; concur again with Mode A and round 0's own disposition -- non-blocking, orchestrator-reconciliation duty at Mode B remains the correct, best-achievable binding for this contract's scope.

A3 (flat verify-evidence-file ordering hazard, Mode A) CARRIES UNCHANGED: design_notes.evidence_vs_reconciliation_split is verbatim-unchanged from round 0, and the bounded tolerate-list (design_notes.tolerate_list_design_and_self_reference_friction) still has no allowance for the flat .agent/evidence/043-verify-NN.txt convention (now NN 01-19, up from 01-15, same underlying hazard). Re-confirmed the underlying CLI mechanism is unchanged (tools/ecosystem_preflight.py untouched, per non_goals) so a stray flat verify-evidence file present during either real dogfood invocation still breaks the run exactly as demonstrated in round 0. Non-blocking, as in round 0, since it fails loudly and is a sequencing-discipline matter for the generator/orchestrator, not a defect in the verification logic.

A2 HARDENING CONFIRMED LANDED AS REQUESTED: design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure now carries an explicit third clause ('(3) DEBT.md BOOKKEEPING (strict ordering...)') pinning that docs/planning/DEBT.md is edited only after both real invocations complete, with the exact mechanical reason (a modified TRACKED file is an unconditional blocking finding no --tolerate-untracked flag can rescue) spelled out in the contract text itself rather than relying solely on out-of-band advisory knowledge. This was recommended, not required, in round 0; its inclusion is a welcome, low-cost hardening.

estimated_lines (130) correctly left unchanged -- the four new verification entries are contract-verification-array growth (evaluated separately from feature-deliverable line count), not feature scope growth; scope.files_create/files_modify and non_goals are otherwise byte-identical to round 0 apart from the copy-to-move wording correction already covered above.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain=v1` checked on the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework before and after this round-2 review; shows only the two pre-existing untracked entries (.agent/contracts/043.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) plus this evaluator's own two review artifacts (.agent/qa/043-contract-review.json from round 0, .agent/qa/043-contract-review-rev1.json from this round), unchanged throughout. All adversarial probes (carve-out, subset-direction, move-reverse, full 19-entry sweep) were built and executed exclusively inside disposable `mktemp -d` / `cp -a` clones under the evaluator's scratchpad; no real repository path under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any other .agent/ location was created, edited, or deleted by this review, and every --operator-approver/--operator-authorization value used was a clearly-labeled test string ('jane', 'alice.chen') inside disposable fixtures only -- no operator approval was ever presented as real.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. Both round-0 blocking findings are genuinely closed, not merely superficially patched: (1) the HIGH exception-set-equality gap is closed by new entry 5 (mechanism-proof, both a clean-equality PASS case and a drifted-approval FAIL case) plus new GATE-DEPENDENT entry 12 (real-artifact counterpart) -- independently re-verified with this evaluator's own from-scratch reconstruction of the original drift scenario (still correctly caught), a carve-out probe (a second, illegitimate extra riding alongside the one legitimate self-reference-directory carve-out is still correctly flagged), and a subset-direction probe (an operator-presented exception silently vanishing from the approved set is also correctly caught, since the fix's second assertion enforces exact set equality, not merely a one-directional superset check). (2) The MEDIUM copy-vs-duplicate contradiction is resolved cleanly to MOVE semantics, consistently across all three design_notes sections and scope.files_create, pinned by new entry 4 (mechanism-proof) and new GATE-DEPENDENT entry 15 (real-artifact counterpart) -- independently re-verified in the reverse direction (a residual duplicate artifact, simulating the exact round-0 defect, is correctly caught as a failure). A full 19-entry sweep (extracted verbatim via json.load, never transcribed) against a fresh disposable clone confirms the generator's own claimed split exactly: 13 mechanism/structural entries pass pre-implementation, 6 gate-dependent entries correctly and expectedly fail pre-implementation (anti-vacuity, not a defect). Mode A's three advisories (A1 sentinel-fabrication limitation, A2 DEBT.md-ordering, A3 flat-evidence-file-ordering) all carry unchanged and remain correctly non-blocking, with A2 now additionally hardened into the contract text itself as a welcome low-cost improvement (recommended, not required, in round 0). No new issues, missing coverage, or unsupported claims found in this round's adversarial probing. Fit for implementation as-is; no further contract revision required before proceeding to the real, gated dogfood run under H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION.
